jokes.text: Dark red. Wrong, but there's a connect-the-dots in here for you later.
jokes.setup: Chandler gives an incorrect crossword answer
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically offers him a children's activity as consolation
jokes.explanation: Clever insult with good economy. The 'connect-the-dots' is an unexpected and specific put-down that fits Monica's character.

jokes.text: Hey, how about maroon? Yes! You are so smart!
jokes.setup: Monica just insulted Chandler's intelligence
jokes.punchline: She immediately praises Rachel for the same type of answer with exaggerated enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's favoritism and double standard. Simple but effective character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Three-letter word. Not dog, but... Cat. You are so smart!
jokes.setup: Monica sets up the most obvious crossword clue possible
jokes.punchline: She still gushes over Chandler's obvious answer, mirroring her earlier praise of Rachel
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier praise, but the setup is almost too obvious. Works through repetition and Monica's character consistency.

jokes.text: Nickel... Okay, dime. You guys keep talking. This could take a while.
jokes.setup: They need a couple dollars for a tip
jokes.punchline: Joey offers increasingly small denominations, Monica realizes this will take forever
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his mathematical obliviousness is well-established. Good escalation from nickel to dime.

jokes.text: Cool. But why would a cop come in here? They don't serve doughnuts.
jokes.setup: Finding a police badge in Central Perk
jokes.punchline: Joey makes the stereotypical cops-and-doughnuts connection
jokes.explanation: Standard stereotype joke but delivered with good timing. Not original but functional.

jokes.text: Could you discover the badge again? I can do better than that.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wants to redo her reaction to the badge discovery
jokes.punchline: Monica promises an even better reaction on the second take
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment about performing reactions. Monica's competitiveness extends even to casual reactions.

jokes.text: My friend's been number 11 forever. This could be her year!
jokes.setup: Phoebe wants to check the 10 Most Wanted List at police station
jokes.punchline: She's optimistic her friend might get promoted from 11th to the top 10
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe line - dark subject matter treated with bizarre optimism. The idea of rooting for someone to move up the Most Wanted list is genuinely funny and character-specific.

jokes.text: It's just that if you wear someone's sweatshirt... shouldn't it be your boyfriend's? And I'm not him.
jokes.setup: Monica is wearing Joey's sweatshirt casually
jokes.punchline: Joey points out the romantic implications with awkward directness
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Joey moment. His inability to handle casual intimacy with friends creates natural tension.

jokes.text: Do I smell bad? You smell like a meadow.
jokes.setup: Monica worries she smells bad
jokes.punchline: Joey gives an unexpectedly poetic and innocent compliment
jokes.explanation: 'Like a meadow' is an unexpectedly sweet and specific compliment from Joey. Sets up the next joke well.

jokes.text: The last time Joey went to a meadow his mother was shot by a hunter.
jokes.setup: Joey's meadow comment and his subsequent discomfort
jokes.punchline: Chandler provides a darkly absurd backstory explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected. The specificity of 'shot by a hunter' and the absurd tragedy makes this memorable. Classic Chandler wit.

jokes.text: What's that doing here? I don't see any doughnuts.
jokes.setup: Monica shows the police badge
jokes.punchline: Rachel makes the same cops-and-doughnuts joke Joey made earlier
jokes.explanation: Lazy callback - just repeating the same joke. Sets up the next bit but isn't funny on its own.

jokes.text: Cops and doughnuts. Come on. If Chandler had said it you'd all be on the floor.
jokes.setup: Rachel gets no reaction to the same joke Joey made
jokes.punchline: Joey complains about the group's bias toward Chandler's humor
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the group dynamics and joke hierarchy. Joey's insecurity about his comedic abilities is well-established and this plays into it perfectly.

jokes.text: Come here to me. What? You say that to kids? The 'Come here to me' is, you know, for the ladies.
jokes.setup: Ross explains his couch should say 'Kids welcome here' and 'Come here to me'
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out how creepy that sounds, Ross clarifies awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Ross's social awkwardness and Rachel's practical reaction work well together.

jokes.text: Ross, honey, it's a nice couch, it's not a magic couch.
jokes.setup: Ross thinks his couch will attract women and be kid-friendly
jokes.punchline: Rachel deflates his expectations with matter-of-fact reality
jokes.explanation: 'Magic couch' is a great phrase that captures Ross's delusions perfectly. Good deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Something didn't quite add up there.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel insist they're not together while clearly being intimate
jokes.punchline: Delivery guy's understated observation of the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor. The delivery guy serves as audience surrogate pointing out the obvious.

jokes.text: Well, you. Her. I mean, she's very... you know. And you're, like... you know?
jokes.setup: Guy tries to explain why Ross and Rachel seem mismatched
jokes.punchline: His tactless attempt to point out Rachel is out of Ross's league
jokes.explanation: Awkward but predictable. The joke relies on established Ross-Rachel dynamic rather than fresh insight.

jokes.text: Not only did we go out... we did it 298 times!
jokes.setup: Ross defends that he and Rachel dated
jokes.punchline: He escalates with an oddly specific and high number
jokes.explanation: The specificity of '298 times' is funny and very Ross-like. His need to prove himself with precise data is character-consistent.

jokes.text: You kept count? You are such a loser!
jokes.setup: Ross reveals the specific count of their intimate encounters
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately calls out how pathetic keeping count is
jokes.explanation: Natural reaction to Ross's revelation. Good chemistry between the characters' established dynamic.

jokes.text: A loser you did it with 298 times!
jokes.setup: Rachel calls Ross a loser
jokes.punchline: Ross turns it around to implicate her in the 'loser' behavior
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure. Ross turns Rachel's insult back on her effectively, using her own participation against her.

jokes.text: Hold it! N.Y.P.D. Freeze, punk! That's right. You are so busted.
jokes.setup: Woman puts cigarette out on tree
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's over-the-top cop roleplay with clichéd police dialogue
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's enthusiasm for playing cop fits her character. The over-the-top delivery of cop clichés works.

jokes.text: Book them. Who are you talking to? Save it, red!
jokes.setup: Phoebe continues her cop roleplay
jokes.punchline: Woman points out she's talking to nobody, Phoebe stays in character with 'Save it, red!'
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment. Phoebe's commitment to the bit despite its obvious flaws is funny. 'Save it, red!' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Backup! Backup! I'm sorry! Sorry! Okay, cancel backup! Cancel backup!
jokes.setup: Phoebe threatens to call for backup
jokes.punchline: The woman immediately caves and apologizes, Phoebe quickly cancels backup
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and quick resolution. The rapid-fire 'cancel backup' adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: It's true. I saw it on Dateline. Jane Pauley is the one woman I would kiss.
jokes.setup: Rachel confirms Ross's paranoid theory about movers
jokes.punchline: She randomly declares her attraction to Jane Pauley
jokes.explanation: Random non-sequitur that works because it's so unexpected. Jane Pauley is an oddly specific choice which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Left. Left. Okay, you know what? There's no more left left.
jokes.setup: Ross is directing Rachel to go left while moving the couch
jokes.punchline: Rachel's frustrated observation about running out of 'left left'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that captures the frustration of the moving situation. 'No more left left' is a nice turn of phrase.

jokes.text: This guy was all: And I'm all, 'Buffay! Homicide!'
jokes.setup: Phoebe recounting her cop adventures
jokes.punchline: She presents herself as a homicide detective with authority
jokes.explanation: 'Buffay! Homicide!' is a great line. Phoebe's fantasy escalation to homicide detective is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'd better take it back. I'm totally drunk with power.
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes she could get arrested for impersonating a cop
jokes.punchline: She admits to being intoxicated by the authority
jokes.explanation: 'Drunk with power' is a classic phrase that works well here. Phoebe's self-awareness about her power trip is funny.

jokes.text: What if Martin Luther King had said that? I kind of have a dream. I don't want to talk about it.
jokes.setup: Joey doesn't want to discuss his dream
jokes.punchline: Chandler imagines MLK being similarly reluctant about his famous dream
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Taking MLK's iconic speech and making it mundane is genuinely clever and unexpected. The structure is perfect.

jokes.text: There was no sex. I haven't dreamt of her like that... since I found out about you two. More or less.
jokes.setup: Joey clarifies his dream about Monica wasn't sexual
jokes.punchline: The 'more or less' qualifier undermines his denial
jokes.explanation: The 'more or less' completely undercuts his claim and creates awkward tension. Good character moment for Joey's honesty.

jokes.text: Or it could mean you saw Chandler and me being close and stuff... and you want that too. Sounds like your dream.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests Joey wants emotional closeness
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically suggests Monica is describing her own fantasy
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback but not particularly sharp. Chandler's deflection is in character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Well, sure! I'm just wearing sweats!
jokes.setup: Joey says he's not attracted to Monica right now
jokes.punchline: Monica takes offense that her casual attire makes her unattractive
jokes.explanation: Good Monica moment - her vanity and competitiveness extend even to being unattractively dressed. Character-consistent.

jokes.text: Friends first? That's interesting. You become friends after? No, never done that either.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being friends before dating
jokes.punchline: Joey reveals he's never been friends with someone after dating either
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey characterization - his shallow approach to relationships is revealed through this logical progression. Self-aware humor.

jokes.text: How you doing?
jokes.setup: Joey encounters Rachel alone in the hallway
jokes.punchline: He delivers his signature pickup line to his friend
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey catchphrase used in a new context. Not original but perfectly executed and character-defining.

jokes.text: In fact, I'm undercover right now. I'm a whore.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claims to be undercover vice cop
jokes.punchline: She matter-of-factly states she's posing as a prostitute
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's matter-of-fact delivery of 'I'm a whore' is perfectly absurd. The casual way she commits to this lie is very her.

jokes.text: Sipowicz? I don't think so. Yeah. Sipowicz. Yeah. Big guy, kind of bald. I don't know him. Don't try to call him. He's not there. He's out. His partner just died.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to name-drop a cop she knows
jokes.punchline: She keeps elaborating the lie with increasingly specific and dramatic details
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of lies. Phoebe's desperation leads to increasingly elaborate backstory. The 'partner just died' is a nice dramatic touch.

jokes.text: I heard that kid from Silver Spoons is really good.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Sipowicz and his deceased partner
jokes.punchline: Cop confuses NYPD Blue with Silver Spoons casting
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference humor. Mixing up Ricky Schroder's shows is a decent period-specific joke but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Think I'll get me one of those. What's up, Joe? I saw you first, so...
jokes.setup: Joey admires Monica and Chandler's relationship
jokes.punchline: He propositions Rachel based solely on seeing her before Phoebe
jokes.explanation: Joey's transactional approach to friendship and romance is perfectly captured. 'I saw you first' is hilariously reductive.

jokes.text: Man, I wish I saw Phoebe first.
jokes.setup: Rachel rejects Joey's proposition
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately regrets his choice based on order of seeing
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the absurd 'saw you first' logic. Shows Joey's shallow decision-making process consistently.

jokes.text: Certainly think a lot of yourself.
jokes.setup: Rachel looks at Ross's sketch showing a large representation
jokes.punchline: She misinterprets his drawing as ego rather than anatomy
jokes.explanation: Standard visual misunderstanding joke. Sets up the clarification nicely but isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: I see. I thought you just really, really liked your new couch.
jokes.setup: Ross clarifies the drawing shows his arm
jokes.punchline: Rachel doubles down on the sexual misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Follows up the visual gag appropriately. Rachel's persistence with the innuendo is mildly funny but predictable.

jokes.text: Pivot! Pivot!
jokes.setup: Ross, Rachel and Chandler trying to get couch up stairs
jokes.punchline: Ross repeatedly shouts 'Pivot!' as his solution to every obstacle
jokes.explanation: Iconic Friends moment. Ross's obsession with 'pivot' as the solution became a cultural touchstone. Perfect economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Shut up! Shut up! Shut up!
jokes.setup: Ross keeps shouting 'Pivot!'
jokes.punchline: Chandler finally snaps and yells at Ross to shut up repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the pivot sequence. Chandler's explosion is exactly what the audience is feeling. Great timing.

jokes.text: What did you mean when you said 'pivot'?
jokes.setup: The couch moving has been a disaster with Ross shouting 'pivot'
jokes.punchline: Rachel asks for clarification of the obvious word
jokes.explanation: Nice capper to the pivot sequence. Rachel's deadpan confusion adds to the absurdity of Ross's commands.

jokes.text: Yeah, because it's not 1985.
jokes.setup: Ross can't figure out why his couch plan didn't work
jokes.punchline: Chandler dates the sketch method as hopelessly outdated
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler line. The specific year reference adds punch and his sarcasm is well-deployed.

jokes.text: Maybe we'll see you on World's Most Boring Police Chases.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says the cop didn't really chase her
jokes.punchline: Chandler references the TV show format with a boring twist
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on the popular TV show format. Good specificity and Chandler's delivery would sell this well.

jokes.text: I am not going back to that hellhole.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains why she ran from the cop
jokes.punchline: She references prison as a 'hellhole' she won't return to
jokes.explanation: Adds to Phoebe's mysterious criminal past. 'Hellhole' is a nice strong word choice that fits her dramatic tendencies.

jokes.text: Suddenly, through no fault of my own, I became irresistible to her! And her roommate.
jokes.setup: Joey tried Rachel's advice about being friends first
jokes.punchline: His 'sincere' approach backfired spectacularly by working too well
jokes.explanation: Great Joey moment. The idea that sincerity becomes a pickup line for him is perfectly character-consistent. The escalation to both roommates is classic Joey.

jokes.text: Closeness, schmoseness. There was three of us, for crying out loud!
jokes.setup: Chandler asks about the emotional closeness Joey wanted
jokes.punchline: Joey dismisses intimacy in favor of his sexual success
jokes.explanation: 'Closeness, schmoseness' is a great dismissive phrase. Joey's priorities completely shifting is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If I go down, you guys go down with me. Harboring a fugitive? That's one to three years minimum.
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizes the cop found her
jokes.punchline: She immediately threatens to implicate her friends with specific legal knowledge
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's criminal knowledge and willingness to throw her friends under the bus is funny and character-consistent.

jokes.text: You're the prettiest fake undercover whore I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Gary is asking Phoebe out on a date
jokes.punchline: His romantic compliment references her fake prostitute persona
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd romantic line. The specificity of 'prettiest fake undercover whore' is genuinely funny and memorable.

jokes.text: He has a gun!
jokes.setup: Gary mentions not taking Phoebe out just for doughnuts
jokes.punchline: Monica suddenly panics about Gary being armed
jokes.explanation: Monica's delayed panic reaction is in character. Simple but effective physical comedy moment.

jokes.text: This couch is cut in half. I would like to exchange it for one that is not cut in half.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to return the destroyed couch
jokes.punchline: He matter-of-factly requests a non-destroyed replacement
jokes.explanation: Ross's logical approach to an absurd situation is funny. The specific phrasing 'not cut in half' is nicely understated.

jokes.text: Look, I'm a reasonable man. I will accept store credit.
jokes.setup: Store won't accept the cut couch
jokes.punchline: Ross presents accepting store credit as a generous compromise
jokes.explanation: Ross's self-perception as reasonable while making unreasonable demands is perfect character work.

jokes.text: I will take it.
jokes.setup: Store offers four dollars store credit
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately accepts the insulting offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to Ross's negotiation. His immediate acceptance after positioning himself as reasonable is great character consistency.
